01_initial_request.input_data: Wireless Sensor Networks (WSNs) infrastructure and sensor node parameters
01_initial_request.user_intent: To improve clustering quality through optimal cluster head selection and advanced cluster formation technique to overcome imbalanced load distribution and uneven energy consumption

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Wireless sensor networks suffer from limited energy supply and premature node death due to imbalanced load distribution and uneven energy consumption. [Gap] Existing clustering protocols like LEACH and YSGAP have issues with cluster head selection accuracy and cluster formation quality. [Decision] To achieve optimal cluster head selection, chose to use Raccoon Optimization Algorithm (ROA) to perform cluster head selection on sensor nodes, expecting to obtain energy-efficient CHs with improved cluster segregation.

parameters.populations: Reachable Zone Population (RZP) and Visible Zone Population (VZP)
parameters.search_zones: RZR (Reachable zone radius) and VZR (Visible zone radius)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: ROA performs accurate search process to select optimal CH based on fitness function that improves clusters segregation

references.relevance: Original ROA algorithm development and characteristics

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ROA requires specific parameter configuration for optimal CH selection performance. [Gap] Parameter values need to be tuned for best FND results. [Decision] To achieve optimal performance, chose to use experimental parameter tuning to perform ROA parameter optimization on protocol configuration, expecting to obtain best FND values.

parameters.MF: fixed for first three cases then evaluated
parameters.RZR: fixed for first three cases then evaluated
parameters.VZR: fixed for first three cases then evaluated
parameters.N_loop: fixed for first three cases then evaluated

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Parameter combination from line 3 of Table 3 performs better in terms of FND than other values tested

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CH selection requires evaluating candidate nodes based on multiple criteria. [Gap] Need fitness function to measure suitability of candidate solutions for CH selection. [Decision] To achieve optimal CH selection, chose to use fitness function to perform CH candidate evaluation on sensor nodes, expecting to obtain quality metrics for CH suitability.

parameters.f2: node density in area (d(node_s, node_j) Euclidean distance)
parameters.f3: distance from new CH to centroid c_k of previously formed clusters

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Fitness function considers residual energy, density around CH, and distance from new CH to previous clusters centroids

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] After CH selection, cluster members need to be assigned to CHs. [Gap] Classical cluster formation leads to inconsistent clusters and overloaded CHs. [Decision] To achieve balanced cluster formation, chose to use multi-objective optimization technique to perform cluster member selection on unclustered nodes, expecting to obtain consistent and efficient cluster composition.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: CH selects cluster members using multi-objective optimization considering distance from CH, residual energy, and distance to other clusters

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Initial matrix needs to be normalized for proper comparison across criteria. [Gap] Raw matrix values need standardization for cost calculation. [Decision] To achieve proper normalization, chose to use matrix normalization process to perform value standardization on initial matrix IM_ch, expecting to obtain normalized matrix NM_ch.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: NM_ch calculated from IM_ch with normalized values for each criterion

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Normalized matrix needs weights to prioritize different criteria. [Gap] Need to assign importance to each selection criterion. [Decision] To achieve weighted cost calculation, chose to use cost matrix construction to perform weight assignment on normalized matrix, expecting to obtain cost_ch_k matrix for member selection.

parameters.weight_matrix: user-defined values based on application requirements
parameters.priority: minimizing intra-cluster distance and improving cluster separation

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cost matrix constructed from multiplying normalized matrix to weight matrix with authors' recorded best results

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cluster members need to be selected based on cost matrix values. [Gap] Need to determine which nodes become cluster members. [Decision] To achieve cluster formation, chose to use cluster member selection algorithm to perform member assignment on cost matrix, expecting to obtain l cluster members with highest cost.

parameters.cluster_size: relative to number of alive nodes in network

02_agent_trajectory.observation: CH selects l cluster members with highest cost and notifies base station about cluster members

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protocol needs to be validated through simulation. [Gap] Need simulation environment to test protocol performance. [Decision] To achieve protocol validation, chose to use OMNET++ simulator with INET 4.0 platform to perform protocol simulation on WSN infrastructure, expecting to obtain performance metrics for comparison.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: ROA-MOCT developed and simulated using OMNET++ simulator tool and INET 4.0 platform

references.citation: [41] Wehrle K, Günes M, Gross J (2010)
[42] Mészáros L, Varga A, Kirsche M (2019)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protocol performance needs to be compared against existing protocols. [Gap] Need baseline protocols for performance comparison. [Decision] To achieve performance validation, chose to use LEACH and YSGAP protocols to perform comparative analysis on network metrics, expecting to obtain relative performance measurements.

parameters.clustering_approach: randomly selects CHs and forms RSSI-based clusters

02_agent_trajectory.observation: LEACH used as baseline comparison protocol with random CH selection

references.citation: [18] Heinzelman WR, Chandrakasan A, Balakrishnan H (2000)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Need another recent protocol for comprehensive comparison. [Gap] Single baseline insufficient for thorough validation. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive comparison, chose to use YSGAP protocol to perform additional comparative analysis on network metrics, expecting to obtain relative performance against recent optimization-based protocol.

parameters.clustering_approach: Yellow Saddle Goatfish Algorithm for optimal CH selection

02_agent_trajectory.observation: YSGAP used as second comparison protocol with optimization-based CH selection

references.citation: [36] Rodríguez A, Del-Valle-Soto C, Velázquez R (2020)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Network lifetime is key metric for WSN protocols. [Gap] Need to measure protocol impact on node longevity. [Decision] To achieve lifetime evaluation, chose to use network lifetime metrics to perform durability assessment on all three protocols, expecting to obtain FND, HND, and LND measurements.

parameters.FND: First Node Death - time when first node exhausted energy
parameters.HND: Half Node Death - time when half nodes exhausted energy
parameters.LND: Last Node Death - time when remaining alive node depleted energy

02_agent_trajectory.observation: ROA-MOCT reaches 1060 rounds vs 298 (LEACH) and 450 (YSGAP) for LND

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Throughput indicates data delivery capability. [Gap] Need to measure packet delivery performance. [Decision] To achieve throughput evaluation, chose to use throughput measurement to perform data delivery assessment on all three protocols, expecting to obtain number of received packets at BS.

parameters.metric: total number of received packets at the BS

02_agent_trajectory.observation: ROA-MOCT outperforms LEACH and YSGAP in network throughput with better clustering quality

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Energy efficiency is critical for WSN longevity. [Gap] Need to measure residual energy over time. [Decision] To achieve energy evaluation, chose to use network residual energy measurement to perform energy consumption assessment on all three protocols, expecting to obtain total remaining energy per round.

parameters.measurement: total remaining energy in the network per round

02_agent_trajectory.observation: ROA-MOCT shows higher residual energy than LEACH and YSGAP by minimizing intra-cluster energy dissipation

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Simulation-based comparative analysis using OMNET++ with INET 4.0 platform against LEACH and YSGAP protocols

network_lifetime_LND.interpretation: ROA-MOCT achieves 1060 rounds vs 450 (YSGAP) and 298 (LEACH), representing 135% improvement over YSGAP and 256% over LEACH

first_node_death_FND.interpretation: 75% of FND values for ROA-MOCT are between 360-405 rounds vs 214-230 (YSGAP) and 89-109 (LEACH)

half_node_death_HND.interpretation: HND values for ROA-MOCT distributed in [980, 1000] vs [381, 400] (YSGAP) and [129, 170] (LEACH)

throughput.interpretation: ROA-MOCT increases number of data packets received by BS due to better clustering quality and improved data flow

residual_energy.interpretation: ROA-MOCT shows higher residual energy by minimizing intra-cluster distance during cluster formation

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The ROA-MOCT protocol successfully achieves the research objective of improving clustering quality through optimal cluster head selection and advanced cluster formation technique. The protocol outperforms both LEACH and YSGAP in terms of network lifetime (FND, HND, LND), throughput, and network residual energy, demonstrating effective overcoming of imbalanced load distribution and uneven energy consumption issues.
